73: ## PRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from kb context)
74: 
75: **Query:** "extract serve agents"
76: 
77: ### Constraints (MUST respect)
78: - Concurrent agents trigger TPM throttling at >60% session usage
79:   - Reason: Observed performance degradation and user reports of hitting limits during swarm operations
80: - Session idle ≠ agent complete
81:   - Reason: Agents legitimately go idle during normal operation (loading, thinking, tool execution)
82: - orch-go agent state exists in four layers (OpenCode memory, OpenCode disk, registry, tmux)
83:   - Reason: Each layer has independent lifecycle - cleanup must touch all layers or ghosts accumulate
84: - Agents must not spawn more than 3 iterations without human review
85:   - Reason: Prevents runaway iteration loops like 12 tmux fallback tests in 9 minutes
86: - orch complete must verify SYNTHESIS.md exists and is not placeholder before closing issue
87:   - Reason: 70% of agents completed without synthesis in 24h chaos period
88: - orch status can show phantom agents (tmux windows where OpenCode exited)
89:   - Reason: No reconciliation between tmux liveness and OpenCode session state
90: - orch status counts ALL workers-* tmux windows as active
91:   - Reason: Discovered during phantom agent investigation - status inflated by persistent windows
92: - orch-knowledge repo is at ~/orch-knowledge (not ~/Documents/personal/orch-knowledge)
93:   - Reason: Agents kept failing to find it when given relative path. Skill sources live at ~/orch-knowledge/skills/src/worker/{skill}/.skillc/
94: - D.E.K.N. 'Next:' field must be updated when marking Status: Complete
95:   - Reason: Prevents stale investigations that mislead future agents
96: - Dashboard event panels max-h-64 for visibility without overwhelming layout
97:   - Reason: Doubled from 32px provides better event scanning while preserving agent grid visibility
98: 
99: ### Prior Decisions
100: - kb search and rg are complementary tools
101:   - Reason: kb searches knowledge artifacts (.kb/), rg searches code - agents should use both strategically based on query type
102: - Headless Swarm = batch execution + rate-limit management across accounts
103:   - Reason: User clarified scope: focus on concurrent agent spawning with capacity management, not distributed architecture or multi-model routing
104: - orch spawn context delivery is reliable
105:   - Reason: Verified that SPAWN_CONTEXT.md is correctly populated and accessible by the agent
106: - Refactor shell-out functions for testability
107:   - Reason: Extracting command construction into Build*Command functions allows unit testing of logic without side effects
108: - orch-go CLI independence
109:   - Reason: CLI commands connect directly to OpenCode (4096), not orch serve (3333)
110: - Use build/orch for serve daemon
111:   - Reason: Prevents SIGKILL during make install
112: - Session ID resolution pattern
113:   - Reason: Commands that need to find agents should use resolveSessionID or the runTail pattern: workspace files first, then API lookup, then tmux fallback
114: - Dual-mode architecture (tmux for visual, HTTP for programmatic) is the correct design
115:   - Reason: Investigation confirmed each mode serves distinct, irreplaceable needs
116: - Use inline lineage metadata for project extraction, not centralized registry
117:   - Reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; centralized registry creates fragile external dependency
118: - orch status shows PHASE and TASK columns from beads data
119:   - Reason: Makes output actionable - users can immediately see what each agent is doing
120:

148: - Agent Lifecycle State Model
149:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/agent-lifecycle-state-model.md
150:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
151:     This model was last updated 2026-02-13.
152:     Changed files: cmd/orch/serve_agents.go, pkg/verify/check.go, .beads/issues.jsonl.
153:     Deleted files: pkg/session/registry.go.
154:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
155:   - Summary:
156:     Agent state exists across **four independent layers** (tmux windows, OpenCode in-memory, OpenCode on-disk, beads comments). These layers fall into two distinct categories: **state layers** (beads, workspace files) that represent what work was done, and **infrastructure layers** (OpenCode sessions, tmux windows) that represent transient execution resources. The dashboard reconciles these via a **Priority Cascade**: check beads issue status first (highest authority), then Phase comments, then registry state, then session existence. Status can appear "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual layer - this is a measurement artifact from combining multiple sources of truth.
157:     
158:     ---
159:   - Critical Invariants:
160:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
161:     2. **Beads issue closed = canonical completion** - All status queries defer to beads
162:     3. **Session existence ≠ agent still working** - Sessions persist indefinitely
163:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
164:     5. **Multiple sources must be reconciled** - No single source has complete truth
165:     6. **Tmux windows are UI layer only** - Not authoritative for state
166:     
167:     ---
168:   - Why This Fails:
169:     ### Failure Mode 1: Dashboard Shows "Active" When Agent is Done
170:     
171:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows agent as active, but `bd show <id>` says status=closed
172:     
173:     **Root cause:** Dashboard caching or SSE lag - hasn't received beads update yet
174:     
175:     **Why it happens:**
176:     
177:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
178:     - `orch complete` closes beads issue
179:     - Beads issue status = closed
180:     - Dashboard hasn't refreshed or polled beads yet
181:     - Dashboard still shows cached "active" state
182:     
183:     **Fix:** Refresh dashboard browser tab (forces beads query)
184:     
185:     **NOT the fix:** Deleting OpenCode session (treats symptom, not cause)
186:     
187:     ### Failure Mode 2: "Dead" Agents That Actually Completed
188:     
189:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
190:     
191:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happened before dashboard queried, cascade reached session check
192:     
193:     **Why it happens:**
194:     
195:     - Agent completed, beads issue closed
196:     - Session cleanup ran (manual or automatic)
197:     - Dashboard cascade: beads check → no issue (closed) → session check → no session → "dead"
198:     
199:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts beads/Phase check before session existence check
200:     
201:     ### Failure Mode 3: Agent Went Idle But Not Complete
202:     
203:     **Symptom:** Session status is "idle" but no `Phase: Complete` comment
204:     
205:     **Root cause:** Agent ran out of context, crashed, or didn't follow completion protocol
206:     
207:     **Why it happens:**
208:     
209:     - Session exhausts context (150k tokens)
210:     - Agent stops responding
211:     - SSE event: `session.status = idle`
212:     - No `Phase: Complete` was ever written
213:     - Dashboard shows "idle" or "waiting"
214:     
215:     **This is expected behavior.** Session idle ≠ work complete. Only agents that explicitly run `bd comment <id> "Phase: Complete"` are considered done.
216:     
217:     **Fix:** Check workspace for what agent accomplished, then either:
218:     
219:     - `orch complete <id> --force` if work is done
220:     - `orch abandon <id>` if work is incomplete
221:     
222:     ### Failure Mode 4: Cross-Project Agents Not Visible
223:     
224:     **Symptom:** Agent spawned with `--workdir /other/project` doesn't appear in dashboard
225:     
226:     **Root cause:** Dashboard only scans current project's `.orch/workspace/` directory
227:     
228:     **Why it happens:**
229:     
230:     - Workspace created in `/other/project/.orch/workspace/`
231:     - Dashboard running from `orch-go` only sees `orch-go/.orch/workspace/`
232:     - Cross-project discovery requires querying OpenCode sessions for unique directories
233:     
234:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Multi-project workspace cache built from OpenCode session directories
235:     
236:     ---
237:   - Your findings should confirm, contradict, or extend the claims above.

258: - Code Extraction Patterns
259:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/extract-patterns.md
260:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
261:     This model was last updated 2026-01-17.
262:     Changed files: cmd/orch/shared.go, cmd/orch/serve_agents.go, cmd/orch/serve_agents_cache.go, cmd/orch/main.go.
263:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
264:   - Summary:
265:     Code extraction is the primary mechanism for **Context Management** in AI-orchestrated environments. Large files (>800 lines) create "Context Noise" that degrades agent performance and increases implementation risk. The system uses a **Phase-based Extraction strategy** (Shared Utilities → Domain Handlers → Sub-domain Infrastructure) to maintain "Cohesive Extraction Units" that fit within a single agent's cognitive window.
266:

269: - Dashboard Agent Status Calculation
270:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/dashboard-agent-status.md
271:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
272:     This model was last updated 2026-01-12.
273:     Deleted files: pkg/status/calculate.go, pkg/dashboard/server.go.
274:     Verify model claims about these files against current code.
275:   - Summary:
276:     The dashboard calculates agent status through a **Priority Cascade model**: check Phase first (highest priority), then registry state, then session existence. Status can be "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual check - this is a measurement artifact, not a system failure. The root issue was observation infrastructure (missing events, double-counting metrics, state not surfaced), not broken completion logic.
277:     
278:     ---
279:   - Critical Invariants:
280:     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
281:     2. **Registry is source of truth for abandonment** - Human judgment, can't be inferred
282:     3. **Session may outlive completion** - Session existence ≠ agent still working
283:     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
284:     
285:     ---
286:   - Why This Fails:
287:     ### Failure Mode 1: "Dead" Agents That Completed
288:     
289:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
290:     
291:     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happens async, dashboard checks session existence as fallback
292:     
293:     **Why it happens:**
294:     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
295:     - `orch complete` verifies and closes beads issue
296:     - Session cleanup happens later (or not at all)
297:     - Dashboard cascade reaches session check → sees no session → "dead"
298:     
299:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts Phase check before session check
300:     
301:     ### Failure Mode 2: Metrics Show Wrong Completion Rate
302:     
303:     **Symptom:** `orch stats` showed 72% completion when reality was 89%
304:     
305:     **Root cause:** Metrics counted events (double-counting) instead of deduplicating entities
306:     
307:     **Why it happens:**
308:     - `agent.completed` event emitted by both `orch complete` AND beads close hook
309:     - Metrics counted events → some completions counted 2x
310:     - Result: inflated total, deflated completion %
311:     
312:     **Fix (Jan 8):** Metrics deduplicate by beads_id before calculating ratios
313:     
314:     ### Failure Mode 3: Work Completed via Bypass Paths
315:     
316:     **Symptom:** Beads issue closed but no completion event, dashboard doesn't update
317:     
318:     **Root cause:** `bd close` (direct) doesn't emit events, only `orch complete` does
319:     
320:     **Why it happens:**
321:     - Multiple paths to completion: `orch complete`, `bd close`, `bd sync` with commit message
322:     - Only `orch complete` emits events
323:     - Other paths are invisible to observation infrastructure
324:     
325:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Beads close hook emits `agent.completed` event
326:     
327:     ---
328:   - Your findings should confirm, contradict, or extend the claims above.
329: - Agent Completion Lifecycle
330:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/completion-lifecycle.md
331:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**

336:   - Summary:
337:     The agent completion lifecycle is the transition from **Active Work** to **Knowledge Persistence**. It is the primary gate for the **Verification Bottleneck**. A healthy lifecycle ensures that agent findings are externalized (D.E.K.N.), workspaces are archived, and OpenCode sessions are purged to prevent "Registry Noise." The system uses a **Phase-based status model** where `Phase: Complete` in Beads is the only authoritative signal for success.
338:

663: ## Constitutional Objection Protocol
664: 
665: **Trigger:** You believe an instruction conflicts with constitutional values (safety, ethics, honesty, user wellbeing) but it's not a clear Hard Limit violation.
666: 
667: **This is DIFFERENT from operational escalation:**
668: 
669: | Type | Examples | Route |
670: |------|----------|-------|
671: | **Operational** | "I'm blocked", "Requirements unclear", "Need decision" | → Orchestrator |
672: | **Constitutional** | "This could harm users", "This feels deceptive", "Ethical concern" | → Human (bypasses orchestrator) |
673: 
674: **Protocol when you have a constitutional concern:**
675: 
676: 1. **Identify the value** - Which constitutional principle is at risk? (safety, honesty, user autonomy, avoiding harm)
677: 
678: 2. **Document it** - `bd comment <id> "CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: [value] - [specific concern]"`
679: 
680: 3. **Do NOT proceed** with the concerning component
681: 
682: 4. **Continue** with unrelated components if the task is separable
683: 
684: 5. **Wait for HUMAN review** - Do not accept orchestrator override on constitutional matters
685: 
686: **Why this bypasses orchestrator:**
687: 
688: Claude's constitution says Claude can refuse unethical instructions regardless of the principal hierarchy. Orchestrators are Claude instances - they cannot authorize constitutional violations any more than you can. Human judgment is required for genuine ethical edge cases.
689: 
690: **Examples:**
691: 
692: | Situation | Response |
693: |-----------|----------|
694: | "Add tracking pixel without disclosure" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: user autonomy - undisclosed tracking |
695: | "Make the unsubscribe button hard to find" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: honesty - dark pattern design |
696: | "Scrape competitor's user data" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: ethics - unauthorized data collection |
697: | "Build feature that targets vulnerable users" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: avoiding harm - exploitation risk |
698: 
699: **When it's NOT a constitutional concern:**
700: - Technical disagreements about implementation
701: - Preference for different architecture
702: - Belief that requirements are suboptimal
703: - Wanting more context before proceeding
704: 
705: These are operational - escalate to orchestrator normally.

821: ### Cross-Repo Issue Handoff
822: 
823: **When you discover an issue that belongs to a different repo**, you cannot create it directly — `bd create` only works in the current project directory, and shell sandboxing prevents `cd` to other repos.
824: 
825: **Instead, output a structured `CROSS_REPO_ISSUE` block** in your beads completion comment or SYNTHESIS.md. The orchestrator will pick this up during completion review and create the issue in the target repo.
826: 
827: **Format:**
828: ```
829: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
830:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/<target-repo>
831:   title: "<concise issue title>"
832:   type: bug|task|feature|question
833:   priority: 0-4
834:   description: "<1-3 sentences with context, evidence, and why it matters>"
835: ```
836: 
837: **Rules:**
838: - Use absolute or `~`-relative paths for `repo`
839: - Include enough context in `description` for the issue to stand alone (the orchestrator in the other repo won't have your session context)
840: - One block per issue — multiple issues get multiple blocks
841: - Report blocks in your `Phase: Complete` comment: `Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block for price-watch`
842: 
843: **Example:**
844: ```bash
845: bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Complete - Implemented token refresh. Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block below.
846: 
847: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
848:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/price-watch
849:   title: Fix ScsOauthClient concurrent token refresh
850:   type: bug
851:   priority: 2
852:   description: During orch-go token handling work, discovered price-watch ScsOauthClient has a race condition when multiple goroutines call RefreshToken simultaneously. No mutex protects the shared token state."
853: ```

953: 
954: # Feature Implementation (Unified Framework)
955: 
956: **For orchestrators:** Spawn via `orch spawn feature-impl "task" --phases "..." --mode ... --validation ...`
957: 
958: **For workers:** You've been spawned to implement a feature using a phased approach with specific configuration.
959: 
960: ---
961: 
962: ## Your Configuration
963: 
964: **Read from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md** to understand your configuration:
965: 
966: - **Phases:** Which phases you'll proceed through (e.g., `investigation,clarifying-questions,design,implementation,validation`)
967: - **Current Phase:** Determined by your progress (start with first configured phase)
968: - **Implementation Mode:** `tdd`, `direct`, or `verification-first` (only relevant if implementation phase included)
969: - **Validation Level:** `none`, `tests`, `smoke-test`, or `multi-phase` (only relevant if validation phase included)
970: 
971: **Example configuration:**
972: ```
973: Phases: design, implementation, validation
974: Mode: tdd
975: Validation: smoke-test
976: ```
977: 
978: **Mode Selection Guide:**
979: 
980: | Mode | When to Use |
981: |------|-------------|
982: | `tdd` | Adding behavior (APIs, business logic, UI) - discover through tests |
983: | `direct` | Non-behavioral changes (refactoring, config, docs) |
984: | `verification-first` | Spec exists, multi-agent work, high-risk features - spec is the contract |
985: 
986: ---

990: | Configuration | Required |
991: |---------------|----------|
992: | investigation phase | Investigation file |
993: | design phase | Design document |
994: | implementation phase | Source code |
995: | mode=tdd | Tests (write first) |
996: | mode=verification-first | Verification spec consumed + tests with AC-xxx traceability + evidence per spec |
997: | validation=tests | Tests |
998: | validation=smoke-test | Validation evidence via bd comment |
999: | validation=multi-phase | Phase checkpoints via bd comment |
1000: 
1001: ---

85: // investigationDirCache holds pre-loaded directory listings for investigation discovery.
86: // This prevents O(n²) behavior when discovering investigation paths for many agents.
87: // Without this cache, each agent would call os.ReadDir() 2-3 times on directories
88: // with 500+ files, resulting in 300+ agents × 500+ files × 2 calls = massive slowdown.
89: type investigationDirCache struct {
90: 	// entries maps directory path -> list of .md file names (not full DirEntry, just names for efficiency)
91: 	entries map[string][]string
92: }
93: 
94: // buildInvestigationDirCache pre-loads directory listings for investigation discovery.
95: // Call this once before processing agents, then pass to discoverInvestigationPath.
96: func buildInvestigationDirCache(projectDirs []string) *investigationDirCache {
97: 	cache := &investigationDirCache{
98: 		entries: make(map[string][]string),
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	for _, projectDir := range projectDirs {
102: 		if projectDir == "" {
103: 			continue
104: 		}
105: 
106: 		// Cache .kb/investigations/
107: 		investigationsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".kb", "investigations")
108: 		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(investigationsDir); err == nil {
109: 			var mdFiles []string
110: 			for _, entry := range entries {
111: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
112: 					mdFiles = append(mdFiles, entry.Name())
113: 				}
114: 			}
115: 			cache.entries[investigationsDir] = mdFiles
116: 		}
117: 
118: 		// Cache .kb/investigations/simple/
119: 		simpleDir := filepath.Join(investigationsDir, "simple")
120: 		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(simpleDir); err == nil {
121: 			var mdFiles []string
122: 			for _, entry := range entries {
123: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
124: 					mdFiles = append(mdFiles, entry.Name())
125: 				}
126: 			}
127: 			cache.entries[simpleDir] = mdFiles
128: 		}
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	return cache
132: }
133: 
134: // getEntries returns cached directory entries, or empty slice if not cached.
135: func (c *investigationDirCache) getEntries(dirPath string) []string {
136: 	if c == nil || c.entries == nil {
137: 		return nil
138: 	}
139: 	return c.entries[dirPath]
140: }
141: 
142: // discoverInvestigationPath attempts to find an investigation file for an agent
143: // using a fallback chain when the agent hasn't reported an investigation_path via beads comment.
144: //
145: // IMPORTANT: Pass a pre-built investigationDirCache to avoid O(n²) directory scanning.
146: // Without the cache, this function would call os.ReadDir() for each agent, causing
147: // massive slowdowns with 300+ agents and 500+ investigation files.
148: //
149: // Fallback chain:
150: // 1. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching workspace name pattern
151: // 2. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching beads ID
152: // 3. Check workspace directory for investigation .md files (excluding SPAWN_CONTEXT.md and SYNTHESIS.md)
153: func discoverInvestigationPath(workspaceName, beadsID, projectDir string, cache *investigationDirCache) string {
154: 	if projectDir == "" {
155: 		return ""
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	// Extract keywords from workspace name for matching (e.g., "og-inv-skillc-deploy-06jan-ed96" -> "skillc-deploy")
159: 	// Workspace names follow pattern: {project}-{skill}-{topic}-{date}-{hash}
160: 	workspaceKeywords := extractWorkspaceKeywords(workspaceName)
161: 
162: 	investigationsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".kb", "investigations")
163: 
164: 	// Use cached entries if available (O(1) lookup vs O(n) ReadDir)
165: 	entries := cache.getEntries(investigationsDir)
166: 	if entries == nil {
167: 		// Fallback to direct read if not cached (shouldn't happen in normal use)
168: 		if dirEntries, err := os.ReadDir(investigationsDir); err == nil {
169: 			for _, entry := range dirEntries {
170: 				if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
171: 					entries = append(entries, entry.Name())
172: 				}
173: 			}
174: 		}
175: 	}
176: 
177: 	// 1. Search for files matching beads ID (e.g., "orch-go-51jz" in filename)
178: 	// This is the most specific match and should be checked first.
179: 	if beadsID != "" {
180: 		// Extract short ID from beads ID (last segment after -)
181: 		shortID := beadsID
182: 		if idx := strings.LastIndex(beadsID, "-"); idx != -1 && idx < len(beadsID)-1 {
183: 			shortID = beadsID[idx+1:]
184: 		}
185: 
186: 		for _, name := range entries {
187: 			// Check if filename contains beads ID or short ID
188: 			if strings.Contains(name, beadsID) || strings.Contains(name, shortID) {
189: 				return filepath.Join(investigationsDir, name)
190: 			}
191: 		}
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// 2. Search .kb/investigations/ for files matching workspace name pattern
195: 	// Workspace names are specific to the agent's task.
196: 	// We reverse the entries list to find the most recent files first (since they are date-prefixed).
197: 	reversedEntries := make([]string, len(entries))
198: 	for i, name := range entries {
199: 		reversedEntries[len(entries)-1-i] = name
200: 	}
201: 
202: 	// First pass: look for exact topic match (highest confidence)
203: 	// We now require at least one keyword match, but we prefer files that match MORE keywords.
204: 	var bestMatch string
205: 	maxMatches := 0
206: 
207: 	for _, name := range reversedEntries {
208: 		matches := 0
209: 		for _, keyword := range workspaceKeywords {
210: 			if keyword != "" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), strings.ToLower(keyword)) {
211: 				matches++
212: 			}
213: 		}
214: 
215: 		if matches > maxMatches {
216: 			maxMatches = matches
217: 			bestMatch = filepath.Join(investigationsDir, name)
218: 			// If we match all keywords, return immediately (highest confidence)
219: 			if matches == len(workspaceKeywords) && len(workspaceKeywords) > 0 {
220: 				return bestMatch
221: 			}
222: 		}
223: 	}
224: 
225: 	if bestMatch != "" {
226: 		return bestMatch
227: 	}
228: 
229: 	// 3. Search for simpler investigations or workspace-specific ones
230: 	if beadsID != "" {
231: 		// Also check .kb/investigations/simple/ (for simpler investigations)
232: 		simpleDir := filepath.Join(investigationsDir, "simple")
233: 		simpleEntries := cache.getEntries(simpleDir)
234: 		if simpleEntries == nil {
235: 			// Fallback to direct read if not cached
236: 			if dirEntries, err := os.ReadDir(simpleDir); err == nil {
237: 				for _, entry := range dirEntries {
238: 					if !entry.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md") {
239: 						simpleEntries = append(simpleEntries, entry.Name())
240: 					}
241: 				}
242: 			}
243: 		}
244: 
245: 		for _, name := range simpleEntries {
246: 			for _, keyword := range workspaceKeywords {
247: 				if keyword != "" && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), strings.ToLower(keyword)) {
248: 					return filepath.Join(simpleDir, name)
249: 				}
250: 			}
251: 		}
252: 	}
253: 
254: 	// 4. Check workspace directory for investigation .md files
255: 	// This is per-workspace so not cached (each workspace is different)
256: 	workspaceDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".orch", "workspace", workspaceName)
257: 	if wsEntries, err := os.ReadDir(workspaceDir); err == nil {
258: 		for _, entry := range wsEntries {
259: 			if entry.IsDir() {
260: 				continue
261: 			}
262: 			name := entry.Name()
263: 			// Skip standard workspace files
264: 			if name == "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md" || name == "SYNTHESIS.md" || name == "ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md" ||
265: 				name == "SESSION_HANDOFF.md" || name == "AGENT_MANIFEST.json" || name == "VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml" ||
266: 				name == ".session_id" || name == ".spawn_time" ||
267: 				name == ".tier" || name == ".beads_id" || name == ".spawn_mode" {
268: 				continue
269: 			}
270: 			// Check for .md files that might be investigation files
271: 			if strings.HasSuffix(name, ".md") && strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(name), "inv") {
272: 				return filepath.Join(workspaceDir, name)
273: 			}
274: 		}
275: 	}
276: 
277: 	return ""
278: }
279: 
280: // extractWorkspaceKeywords extracts meaningful keywords from a workspace name for investigation matching.
281: // Workspace names follow pattern: {project}-{skill}-{topic}-{date}-{hash}
282: // Example: "og-inv-skillc-deploy-06jan-ed96" -> ["skillc", "deploy"]
283: func extractWorkspaceKeywords(workspaceName string) []string {
284: 	parts := strings.Split(workspaceName, "-")
285: 	if len(parts) < 3 {
286: 		return nil
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	var keywords []string
290: 
291: 	// Skip prefix parts that are likely project or skill markers
292: 	skipPrefixes := []string{"og", "inv", "feat", "fix", "debug", "audit", "impl", "arch", "research"}
293: 	prefixSet := make(map[string]bool)
294: 	for _, p := range skipPrefixes {
295: 		prefixSet[p] = true
296: 	}
297: 
298: 	for _, part := range parts {
299: 		// Skip short parts (likely hash or date)
300: 		if len(part) <= 2 {
301: 			continue
302: 		}
303: 		// Skip parts that look like dates (e.g., "06jan", "2026")
304: 		if len(part) == 5 && strings.Contains(part, "jan") || strings.Contains(part, "feb") ||
305: 			strings.Contains(part, "mar") || strings.Contains(part, "apr") ||
306: 			strings.Contains(part, "may") || strings.Contains(part, "jun") ||
307: 			strings.Contains(part, "jul") || strings.Contains(part, "aug") ||
308: 			strings.Contains(part, "sep") || strings.Contains(part, "oct") ||
309: 			strings.Contains(part, "nov") || strings.Contains(part, "dec") {
310: 			continue
311: 		}
312: 		// Skip common prefixes
313: 		if prefixSet[strings.ToLower(part)] {
314: 			continue
315: 		}
316: 		// Skip parts that look like short hashes (4 hex chars at end)
317: 		if len(part) == 4 && isHexLike(part) {
318: 			continue
319: 		}
320: 		keywords = append(keywords, part)
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	return keywords
324: }
325: 
326: // isHexLike returns true if the string looks like a short hex hash (all lowercase letters/digits).
327: func isHexLike(s string) bool {
328: 	for _, c := range s {
329: 		if !((c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')) {
330: 			return false
331: 		}
332: 	}
333: 	return true
334: }
335: 
336: // uniqueProjectDirs deduplicates project directories while preserving order.
337: func uniqueProjectDirs(dirs []string) []string {
338: 	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(dirs))
339: 	result := make([]string, 0, len(dirs))
340: 	for _, dir := range dirs {
341: 		if dir == "" {
342: 			continue
343: 		}
344: 		cleaned := filepath.Clean(dir)
345: 		if seen[cleaned] {
346: 			continue
347: 		}
348: 		seen[cleaned] = true
349: 		result = append(result, cleaned)
350: 	}
351: 	return result
352: }
353: 
354: // listInProgressIssues retrieves in_progress issues across project directories.
355: // Returns a map of beadsID -> Issue, plus beadsID -> projectDir for cross-project lookups.
356: func listInProgressIssues(projectDirs []string) (map[string]*verify.Issue, map[string]string) {
357: 	issues := make(map[string]*verify.Issue)
358: 	projectDirsByID := make(map[string]string)
359: 
360: 	if len(projectDirs) == 0 {
361: 		openIssues, err := listOpenIssues()
362: 		if err != nil {
363: 			log.Printf("Warning: failed to list open issues: %v", err)
364: 			return issues, projectDirsByID
365: 		}
366: 
367: 		for id, issue := range openIssues {
368: 			if strings.EqualFold(issue.Status, "in_progress") {
369: 				issues[id] = issue
370: 			}
371: 		}
372: 
373: 		return issues, projectDirsByID
374: 	}
375: 
376: 	for _, projectDir := range projectDirs {
377: 		openIssues, err := listOpenIssuesWithDir(projectDir)
378: 		if err != nil {
379: 			log.Printf("Warning: failed to list open issues for %s: %v", projectDir, err)
380: 			continue
381: 		}
382: 
383: 		for id, issue := range openIssues {
384: 			if strings.EqualFold(issue.Status, "in_progress") {
385: 				if _, exists := issues[id]; !exists {
386: 					issues[id] = issue
387: 					projectDirsByID[id] = projectDir
388: 				}
389: 			}
390: 		}
391: 	}
392: 
393: 	return issues, projectDirsByID
394: }
395: 
396: // handleAgents returns JSON list of in-progress agents (beads-first) and completed workspaces.
397: // Query parameters:
398: //   - since: Time filter (12h, 24h, 48h, 7d, all). Default: 12h
399: //   - project: Project filter (full path or project name). Default: none (all projects)
400: func handleAgents(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
401: 	if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
402: 		http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
403: 		return
404: 	}
405: 
406: 	// Parse query parameters for filtering
407: 	sinceDuration := parseSinceParam(r)
408: 	projectFilterParam := parseProjectFilter(r)
409: 
410: 	// Use sourceDir (set at build time) since serve may run from any working directory
411: 	projectDir := sourceDir
412: 	if projectDir == "" || projectDir == "unknown" {
413: 		projectDir, _ = os.Getwd()
414: 	}
415: 
416: 	client := opencode.NewClient(serverURL)
417: 
418: 	sessions, err := listSessionsAcrossProjects(client, projectDir)
419: 	if err != nil {
420: 		log.Printf("Warning: failed to list sessions: %v", err)
421: 	}
422: 
423: 	sessionByID := make(map[string]*opencode.Session)
424: 	beadsToSession := make(map[string]*opencode.Session)
425: 	for i := range sessions {
426: 		session := &sessions[i]
427: 		sessionByID[session.ID] = session
428: 
429: 		beadsID := beadsIDFromSession(session)
430: 		if beadsID != "" {
431: 			beadsToSession[beadsID] = session
432: 		}
433: 	}
434: 
435: 	sessionStatusMap := make(map[string]opencode.SessionStatusInfo)
436: 	if status, err := client.GetAllSessionStatus(); err != nil {
437: 		log.Printf("Warning: failed to fetch session status: %v", err)
438: 	} else {
439: 		sessionStatusMap = status
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	projectDirs := uniqueProjectDirs(append([]string{projectDir}, getKBProjectsFn()...))
443: 	wsCache := globalWorkspaceCacheInstance.getCachedWorkspace(projectDirs)
444: 
445: 	agents := []AgentAPIResponse{} // Initialize as empty slice, not nil, to return [] instead of null
446: 
447: 	// Collect beads IDs for batch fetching
448: 	beadsIDsToFetch := make([]string, 0)
449: 	seenBeadsIDs := make(map[string]bool)
450: 
451: 	// Track project directories for cross-project agents
452: 	// Key: beadsID, Value: projectDir from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md or beads query
453: 	beadsProjectDirs := make(map[string]string)
454: 
455: 	// Beads-first discovery: start from in_progress issues
456: 	inProgressIssues, issueProjectDirs := listInProgressIssues(projectDirs)
457: 	for beadsID, projectPath := range issueProjectDirs {
458: 		if projectPath != "" {
459: 			beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = projectPath
460: 		}
461: 	}
462: 
463: 	now := time.Now()
464: 	// Filter: only show sessions updated in the last 10 minutes as "active"
465: 	// Two-threshold ghost filtering:
466: 	// - Active threshold (10min): determines "running" vs "idle" status
467: 	// - Display threshold (4h): filters ghosts from default view (unless Phase: Complete)
468: 	activeThreshold := 10 * time.Minute
469: 	displayThreshold := 4 * time.Hour
470: 
471: 	// Dead threshold: if no activity for 3 minutes, session is dead.
472: 	// Agents are constantly reading, editing, running commands - 3 min silence = dead.
473: 	// This is critical for visibility: dead agents need attention (crashed/stuck/killed).
474: 	deadThreshold := 3 * time.Minute
475: 
476: 	// Stalled threshold: if same phase for 15+ minutes, agent may be stuck.
477: 	// This is advisory only - surfaces in Needs Attention but doesn't auto-abandon.
478: 	// Designed to catch agents that have heartbeat but aren't making progress.
479: 	// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-design-stalled-agent-detection-agents.md
480: 	stalledThreshold := 15 * time.Minute
481: 
482: 	// beadsFetchThreshold limits which completed workspaces we fetch beads data for.
483: 	// This remains important for performance on large workspaces archives.
484: 	beadsFetchThreshold := 2 * time.Hour
485: 	if sinceDuration > beadsFetchThreshold {
486: 		beadsFetchThreshold = sinceDuration
487: 	} else if sinceDuration == 0 {
488: 		// "all" requested
489: 		beadsFetchThreshold = 365 * 24 * time.Hour
490: 	}
491: 
492: 	// Track which agents need post-filtering by beads ID (idle > displayThreshold)
493: 	// These will be filtered out after Phase check unless Phase: Complete
494: 	pendingFilterByBeadsID := make(map[string]bool)
495: 
496: 	for beadsID, issue := range inProgressIssues {
497: 		agent := AgentAPIResponse{
498: 			BeadsID:    beadsID,
499: 			BeadsTitle: issue.Title,
500: 			Task:       truncate(issue.Title, 60),
501: 			Status:     "dead",
502: 			Project:    extractProjectFromBeadsID(beadsID),
503: 			ProjectDir: issueProjectDirs[beadsID],
504: 		}
505: 
506: 		workspacePath := wsCache.lookupWorkspace(beadsID)
507: 		if workspacePath == "" {
508: 			if session, ok := beadsToSession[beadsID]; ok {
509: 				if sessionWorkspace := workspacePathFromSession(session); sessionWorkspace != "" {
510: 					workspacePath = sessionWorkspace
511: 				}
512: 			}
513: 		}
514: 		if workspacePath != "" {
515: 			workspaceName := filepath.Base(workspacePath)
516: 			agent.ID = workspaceName
517: 			agent.Skill = extractSkillFromTitle(workspaceName)
518: 			if sessionID := spawn.ReadSessionID(workspacePath); sessionID != "" {
519: 				agent.SessionID = sessionID
520: 			}
521: 			if spawnTime := spawn.ReadSpawnTime(workspacePath); !spawnTime.IsZero() {
522: 				agent.SpawnedAt = spawnTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
523: 			}
524: 			if agentProjectDir := wsCache.lookupProjectDir(beadsID); agentProjectDir != "" {
525: 				agent.ProjectDir = agentProjectDir
526: 				beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = agentProjectDir
527: 			}
528: 		}
529: 
530: 		if agent.SessionID == "" {
531: 			if session, ok := beadsToSession[beadsID]; ok {
532: 				agent.SessionID = session.ID
533: 			}
534: 		}
535: 
536: 		if agent.Project == "" && agent.ProjectDir != "" {
537: 			agent.Project = extractProjectName(agent.ProjectDir)
538: 		}
539: 
540: 		if agent.SessionID != "" {
541: 			session := sessionByID[agent.SessionID]
542: 			if session == nil {
543: 				var err error
544: 				session, err = client.GetSession(agent.SessionID)
545: 				if err != nil {
546: 					log.Printf("Warning: failed to fetch session %s: %v", agent.SessionID, err)
547: 				}
548: 			}
549: 
550: 			if session != nil {
551: 				if agent.BeadsID == "" {
552: 					agent.BeadsID = beadsIDFromSession(session)
553: 				}
554: 				if agent.ID == "" {
555: 					agent.ID = workspaceNameFromSession(session)
556: 				}
557: 				if agent.ProjectDir == "" {
558: 					if projectDir := projectDirFromWorkspacePath(workspacePathFromSession(session)); projectDir != "" {
559: 						agent.ProjectDir = projectDir
560: 					} else if session.Directory != "" {
561: 						agent.ProjectDir = session.Directory
562: 					}
563: 				}
564: 				if agent.Tier == "" && session.Metadata != nil {
565: 					if tier, ok := session.Metadata["tier"]; ok {
566: 						agent.Tier = tier
567: 					}
568: 				}
569: 
570: 				if agent.ProjectDir != "" {
571: 					if _, exists := beadsProjectDirs[beadsID]; !exists {
572: 						beadsProjectDirs[beadsID] = agent.ProjectDir
573: 					}
574: 				}
575: 
576: 				createdAt := time.Unix(session.Time.Created/1000, 0)
577: 				updatedAt := time.Unix(session.Time.Updated/1000, 0)
578: 				runtime := now.Sub(createdAt)
579: 				timeSinceUpdate := now.Sub(updatedAt)
580: 
581: 				status := "active"
582: 				if timeSinceUpdate > deadThreshold {
583: 					status = "dead"
584: 				} else if timeSinceUpdate > activeThreshold {
585: 					status = "idle"
586: 				}
587: 
588: 				agent.Status = status
589: 				agent.Runtime = formatDuration(runtime)
590: 				if agent.SpawnedAt == "" {
591: 					agent.SpawnedAt = createdAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
592: 				}
593: 				agent.UpdatedAt = updatedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
594: 			}
595: 
596: 			if statusInfo, ok := sessionStatusMap[agent.SessionID]; ok {
597: 				agent.IsProcessing = statusInfo.IsBusy() || statusInfo.IsRetrying()
598: 			}
599: 		}
600: 
601: 		if agent.Status == "idle" && agent.UpdatedAt != "" {
602: 			if updatedAt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, agent.UpdatedAt); err == nil {
603: 				if now.Sub(updatedAt) > displayThreshold {
604: 					pendingFilterByBeadsID[beadsID] = true
605: 				}
606: 			}
607: 		}
608: 
609: 		if !seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] {
610: 			beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsID)
611: 			seenBeadsIDs[beadsID] = true
612: 		}
613: 		agents = append(agents, agent)
614: 	}
615: 
616: 	// Add completed workspaces (those with SYNTHESIS.md or light-tier completions)
617: 	// Reuse cached workspace entries to avoid redundant directory reads
618: 	// Multi-project support: entries may come from different project workspace directories
619: 	if len(wsCache.workspaceEntries) > 0 {
620: 		for _, entry := range wsCache.workspaceEntries {
621: 			if !entry.IsDir() {
622: 				continue
623: 			}
624: 
625: 			workspaceName := entry.Name()
626: 
627: 			// Extract workspacePath and beadsID early for accurate dedup.
628: 			// This catches tmux agents whose IDs include emoji prefix and [beads-id] suffix,
629: 			// preventing the workspace scan from adding a duplicate "completed" entry.
630: 			workspacePath := wsCache.lookupWorkspacePathByEntry(workspaceName)
631: 			wsBeadsID := extractBeadsIDFromWorkspace(workspacePath)
632: 			if wsBeadsID == "" {
633: 				wsBeadsID = extractBeadsIDFromTitle(workspaceName)
634: 			}
635: 
636: 			// Check if already in active list (by name OR beads_id)
637: 			alreadyIn := false
638: 			for _, a := range agents {
639: 				if a.ID == workspaceName || strings.HasPrefix(a.ID, workspaceName+" ") {
640: 					alreadyIn = true
641: 					break
642: 				}
643: 				if wsBeadsID != "" && a.BeadsID == wsBeadsID {
644: 					alreadyIn = true
645: 					break
646: 				}
647: 			}
648: 
649: 			if alreadyIn {
650: 				continue
651: 			}
652: 			synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
653: 			hasSynthesis := false
654: 
655: 			// Check if SYNTHESIS.md exists (indicates full-tier completion)
656: 			if _, err := os.Stat(synthesisPath); err == nil {
657: 				hasSynthesis = true
658: 			}
659: 
660: 			// Only add workspaces that have SYNTHESIS.md for now
661: 			// Light-tier completions will be detected via Phase: Complete in beads comments
662: 			if !hasSynthesis {
663: 				// For light-tier, check if there's a SPAWN_CONTEXT.md (indicates it's a valid spawn)
664: 				spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
665: 				if _, err := os.Stat(spawnContextPath); err != nil {
666: 					continue // Not a valid spawn workspace
667: 				}
668: 			}
669: 
670: 			agent := AgentAPIResponse{
671: 				ID:     entry.Name(),
672: 				Status: "completed",
673: 			}
674: 
675: 			// Set updated_at from workspace name date suffix or file modification time
676: 			// This ensures proper sorting in archive section
677: 			if parsedDate := extractDateFromWorkspaceName(entry.Name()); !parsedDate.IsZero() {
678: 				agent.UpdatedAt = parsedDate.Format(time.RFC3339)
679: 			} else if hasSynthesis {
680: 				// Fallback to file modification time of SYNTHESIS.md
681: 				if info, err := os.Stat(synthesisPath); err == nil {
682: 					agent.UpdatedAt = info.ModTime().Format(time.RFC3339)
683: 				}
684: 			} else {
685: 				// For light-tier, use SPAWN_CONTEXT.md modification time
686: 				spawnContextPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SPAWN_CONTEXT.md")
687: 				if info, err := os.Stat(spawnContextPath); err == nil {
688: 					agent.UpdatedAt = info.ModTime().Format(time.RFC3339)
689: 				}
690: 			}
691: 
692: 			// Read session ID from workspace
693: 			if sessionID := spawn.ReadSessionID(workspacePath); sessionID != "" {
694: 				agent.SessionID = sessionID
695: 			}
696: 
697: 			// Read spawn time from workspace
698: 			if spawnTime := spawn.ReadSpawnTime(workspacePath); !spawnTime.IsZero() {
699: 				agent.SpawnedAt = spawnTime.Format(time.RFC3339)
700: 			}
701: 
702: 			// Parse synthesis (only for full-tier)
703: 			if hasSynthesis {
704: 				if synthesis, err := verify.ParseSynthesis(workspacePath); err == nil {
705: 					agent.Synthesis = &SynthesisResponse{
706: 						TLDR:           synthesis.TLDR,
707: 						Outcome:        synthesis.Outcome,
708: 						Recommendation: synthesis.Recommendation,
709: 						DeltaSummary:   summarizeDelta(synthesis.Delta),
710: 						NextActions:    synthesis.NextActions,
711: 					}
712: 				}
713: 				// Read raw SYNTHESIS.md content for inline rendering in dashboard
714: 				synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
715: 				if content, err := os.ReadFile(synthesisPath); err == nil {
716: 					agent.SynthesisContent = string(content)
717: 				}
718: 			}
719: 
720: 			agent.BeadsID = wsBeadsID
721: 			agent.Skill = extractSkillFromTitle(entry.Name())
722: 
723: 			// Include beads enrichment for recent workspace entries to populate phase data.
724: 			// Performance guard: only enrich workspaces within beadsFetchThreshold to avoid
725: 			// CPU spikes from fetching beads data for 600+ historical workspaces.
726: 			if agent.BeadsID != "" && !seenBeadsIDs[agent.BeadsID] {
727: 				workspaceTime := extractDateFromWorkspaceName(workspaceName)
728: 				if !workspaceTime.IsZero() && now.Sub(workspaceTime) <= beadsFetchThreshold {
729: 					beadsIDsToFetch = append(beadsIDsToFetch, agent.BeadsID)
730: 					seenBeadsIDs[agent.BeadsID] = true
731: 
732: 					// For cross-project agent visibility: use cached PROJECT_DIR
733: 					if agentProjectDir := wsCache.lookupProjectDir(agent.BeadsID); agentProjectDir != "" {
734: 						beadsProjectDirs[agent.BeadsID] = agentProjectDir
735: 					}
736: 				}
737: 			}
738: 
739: 			agents = append(agents, agent)
740: 		}
741: 	}
742: 
743: 	// Batch fetch beads data (phase from comments, task from issues, close_reason for completed)
744: 	// This is the same pattern used by orch status for efficiency.
745: 	// Uses TTL cache to prevent CPU spikes from spawning 20+ bd processes per request.
746: 	if len(beadsIDsToFetch) > 0 {
747: 		// Batch fetch all issues (including closed) for close_reason (cached with TTL)
748: 		// Uses bd show which works for any issue status
749: 		allIssues, _ := globalBeadsCache.getAllIssues(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsProjectDirs)
750: 
751: 		// Batch fetch comments for all beads IDs (cached with TTL)
752: 		// Use project-aware batch fetch for cross-project agent visibility
753: 		commentsMap := globalBeadsCache.getComments(beadsIDsToFetch, beadsProjectDirs)
754: 
755: 		// Build investigation directory cache ONCE before the agent loop.
756: 		// This prevents O(n²) behavior: without this, discoverInvestigationPath would call
757: 		// os.ReadDir() 2-3 times per agent, scanning 500+ files each time.
758: 		// With 300+ agents, that's 300 × 500 × 2 = 300,000+ file comparisons.
759: 		// The cache reduces this to a single ReadDir() call per directory.
760: 		uniqueProjectDirs := make([]string, 0, len(beadsProjectDirs))
761: 		seenDirs := make(map[string]bool)
762: 		for _, dir := range beadsProjectDirs {
763: 			if dir != "" && !seenDirs[dir] {
764: 				seenDirs[dir] = true
765: 				uniqueProjectDirs = append(uniqueProjectDirs, dir)
766: 			}
767: 		}
768: 		invDirCache := buildInvestigationDirCache(uniqueProjectDirs)
769: 
770: 		// Track PhaseReportedAt for completion backlog detection.
771: 		// Populated during the enrichment loop below, used after to detect backlogged agents.
772: 		phaseReportedAtMap := make(map[string]time.Time)
773: 
774: 		// Populate phase, task, close_reason, and status for each agent using Priority Cascade model.
775: 		// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md for design.
776: 		for i := range agents {
777: 			if agents[i].BeadsID == "" {
778: 				continue
779: 			}
780: 
781: 			// Get task from in-progress issue first (beads-first)
782: 			if issue, ok := inProgressIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
783: 				agents[i].Task = truncate(issue.Title, 60)
784: 				if agents[i].BeadsTitle == "" {
785: 					agents[i].BeadsTitle = issue.Title
786: 				}
787: 			}
788: 
789: 			// If not in in-progress issues, try all issues (for closed ones)
790: 			if agents[i].Task == "" {
791: 				if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
792: 					agents[i].Task = truncate(issue.Title, 60)
793: 					if agents[i].BeadsTitle == "" {
794: 						agents[i].BeadsTitle = issue.Title
795: 					}
796: 					// For completed agents without synthesis, use close_reason as fallback
797: 					if agents[i].Synthesis == nil && issue.CloseReason != "" {
798: 						agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
799: 					}
800: 				}
801: 			}
802: 
803: 			// Gather completion signals for Priority Cascade model
804: 			issueClosed := false
805: 			phaseComplete := false
806: 
807: 			// Check if beads issue is closed (Priority 1)
808: 			if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
809: 				issueClosed = strings.EqualFold(issue.Status, "closed")
810: 				// Capture close_reason if available
811: 				if issueClosed && issue.CloseReason != "" && agents[i].CloseReason == "" {
812: 					agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
813: 				}
814: 			}
815: 
816: 			// Get phase from comments (Priority 2)
817: 			if comments, ok := commentsMap[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
818: 				phaseStatus := verify.ParsePhaseFromComments(comments)
819: 				if phaseStatus.Found {
820: 					agents[i].Phase = phaseStatus.Phase
821: 					phaseComplete = strings.EqualFold(phaseStatus.Phase, "Complete")
822: 
823: 					// Track PhaseReportedAt for completion backlog detection and API response
824: 					if phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt != nil {
825: 						phaseReportedAtMap[agents[i].BeadsID] = *phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt
826: 						agents[i].PhaseReportedAt = phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
827: 					}
828: 
829: 					// Stalled detection: if active agent has same phase for 15+ minutes
830: 					// Advisory only - surfaces in Needs Attention but doesn't auto-abandon.
831: 					// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-design-stalled-agent-detection-agents.md
832: 					if agents[i].Status == "active" && phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt != nil {
833: 						timeSincePhase := now.Sub(*phaseStatus.PhaseReportedAt)
834: 						if timeSincePhase > stalledThreshold {
835: 							agents[i].IsStalled = true
836: 						}
837: 					}
838: 				}
839: 				// Extract investigation_path from comments for investigation tab rendering
840: 				if investigationPath := verify.ParseInvestigationPathFromComments(comments); investigationPath != "" {
841: 					agents[i].InvestigationPath = investigationPath
842: 				}
843: 			}
844: 
845: 			// Populate project_dir from beadsProjectDirs lookup (for workspace path construction)
846: 			// Track if we got a reliable project dir from workspace cache (vs using session directory)
847: 			hasReliableProjectDir := false
848: 			if agentProjectDir, ok := beadsProjectDirs[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok {
849: 				agents[i].ProjectDir = agentProjectDir
850: 				hasReliableProjectDir = true
851: 			}
852: 
853: 			// Auto-discover investigation path if not provided via beads comment
854: 			// This uses a fallback chain: .kb/investigations/ matching -> workspace .md files
855: 			// Uses invDirCache to avoid O(n²) directory scanning (built once before this loop)
856: 			// IMPORTANT: Only auto-discover if we have a reliable project dir from workspace cache.
857: 			// The session directory (s.Directory) may be the orchestrator's cwd due to OpenCode --attach bug,
858: 			// which would cause us to search the wrong project's .kb/investigations/ for cross-project agents.
859: 			if agents[i].InvestigationPath == "" && hasReliableProjectDir {
860: 				workspaceName := agents[i].ID
861: 				if idx := strings.Index(workspaceName, " ["); idx != -1 {
862: 					workspaceName = workspaceName[:idx]
863: 				}
864: 				discoveredPath := discoverInvestigationPath(workspaceName, agents[i].BeadsID, agents[i].ProjectDir, invDirCache)
865: 				if discoveredPath != "" {
866: 					agents[i].InvestigationPath = discoveredPath
867: 				}
868: 			}
869: 
870: 			// Read investigation file content for inline rendering in dashboard
871: 			if agents[i].InvestigationPath != "" {
872: 				if content, err := os.ReadFile(agents[i].InvestigationPath); err == nil {
873: 					agents[i].InvestigationContent = string(content)
874: 				}
875: 			}
876: 
877: 			// Get workspace path for SYNTHESIS.md check (Priority 3)
878: 			workspacePath := wsCache.lookupWorkspace(agents[i].BeadsID)
879: 			// Fallback: For untracked agents, try looking up by workspace name from session title
880: 			if workspacePath == "" && agents[i].ID != "" {
881: 				workspaceName := agents[i].ID
882: 				if idx := strings.Index(workspaceName, " ["); idx != -1 {
883: 					workspaceName = workspaceName[:idx]
884: 				}
885: 				workspacePath = wsCache.lookupWorkspacePathByEntry(workspaceName)
886: 			}
887: 
888: 			// Use Priority Cascade to determine final status
889: 			// Priority order: issueClosed > phaseComplete > SYNTHESIS.md > sessionStatus
890: 			agents[i].Status = determineAgentStatus(issueClosed, phaseComplete, workspacePath, agents[i].Status)
891: 
892: 			// For completed agents, also check close_reason if synthesis is null
893: 			if agents[i].Status == "completed" && agents[i].Synthesis == nil && agents[i].CloseReason == "" {
894: 				if issue, ok := allIssues[agents[i].BeadsID]; ok && issue.CloseReason != "" {
895: 					agents[i].CloseReason = issue.CloseReason
896: 				}
897: 			}
898: 
899: 			// Read synthesis content for active agents that have a workspace with SYNTHESIS.md
900: 			// (Completed workspaces already have this populated above in the workspace scan)
901: 			if workspacePath != "" && agents[i].SynthesisContent == "" {

912: 								DeltaSummary:   summarizeDelta(synthesis.Delta),

916: 					}

918: 			}
919: 		}
920: 
921: 		// Fetch gap analysis from spawn events for each agent
922: 		gapAnalysisMap := getGapAnalysisFromEvents(beadsIDsToFetch)
923: 		for i := range agents {

944: 			if status != "active" && status != "idle" && status != "completed" {

951: 			// Get last activity time

967: 	}

969: 	// NOTE: The duplicate SYNTHESIS.md check that was here has been removed.
970: 	// All agents are now included in beadsIDsToFetch, so the Priority Cascade
971: 	// in determineAgentStatus() handles all status determination in one place.
972: 	// See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md
973: 
974: 	// Fetch token usage and last activity for agents with valid session IDs
975: 	// Parallelized to avoid sequential HTTP calls causing ~20s delays with 200+ agents.
976: 	// Uses goroutines with semaphore to limit concurrent requests.
977: 	// Both tokens and activity are extracted from the same GetMessages call for efficiency.
978: 	type sessionResult struct {
979: 		index    int
980: 		tokens   *opencode.TokenStats
981: 		activity *opencode.LastActivity
982: 	}
983: 	resultChan := make(chan sessionResult, len(agents))
984: 
985: 	// Limit concurrent HTTP requests to avoid overwhelming the OpenCode server
986: 	const maxConcurrent = 20
987: 	sem := make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrent)
988: 
989: 	var wg sync.WaitGroup
990: 	for i := range agents {
991: 		// Skip agents without session ID, completed agents, or idle agents.
992: 		// Token data is static for completed agents, and idle agents are unlikely to have changed.
993: 		// This prevents CPU spikes from making HTTP calls to OpenCode for 300+ inactive sessions.
994: 		if agents[i].SessionID == "" || agents[i].Status == "completed" || agents[i].Status == "idle" {
995: 			continue
996: 		}
997: 
998: 		wg.Add(1)
999: 		go func(idx int, sessionID string) {
1000: 			defer wg.Done()
1001: 			sem <- struct{}{}        // Acquire semaphore
1002: 			defer func() { <-sem }() // Release semaphore
1003: 
1004: 			// Fetch messages once and extract both tokens and activity
1005: 			messages, err := client.GetMessages(sessionID)
1006: 			if err != nil || len(messages) == 0 {
1007: 				return
1008: 			}
1009: 
1010: 			result := sessionResult{index: idx}
1011: 
1012: 			// Extract tokens
1013: 			tokenStats := opencode.AggregateTokens(messages)
1014: 			result.tokens = &tokenStats
1015: 
1016: 			// Extract last activity from messages
1017: 			result.activity = extractLastActivityFromMessages(messages)
1018: 
1019: 			resultChan <- result
1020: 		}(i, agents[i].SessionID)
1021: 	}
1022: 
1023: 	// Wait for all goroutines to complete, then close channel
1024: 	go func() {
1025: 		wg.Wait()
1026: 		close(resultChan)
1027: 	}()
1028: 
1029: 	// Collect results and check for stall
1030: 	for result := range resultChan {
1031: 		if result.tokens != nil {
1032: 			agents[result.index].Tokens = result.tokens
1033: 
1034: 			// Check for stall: agent running but no token progress for N minutes
1035: 			// Only check for active agents (skip idle/dead/completed)
1036: 			if agents[result.index].Status == "active" && agents[result.index].SessionID != "" {
1037: 				// Update stall tracker and check if agent is stalled
1038: 				isStalled := globalStallTracker.Update(agents[result.index].SessionID, result.tokens)
1039: 
1040: 				// Set IsStalled if either phase-based OR token-based stall detected
1041: 				// Phase-based stall: same phase for 15+ minutes (already set earlier)
1042: 				// Token-based stall: no token progress for 3+ minutes (checked here)
1043: 				if isStalled {
1044: 					agents[result.index].IsStalled = true
1045: 				}
1046: 			}
1047: 		}
1048: 		if result.activity != nil {
1049: 			agents[result.index].CurrentActivity = result.activity.Text
1050: 			agents[result.index].LastActivityAt = time.Unix(result.activity.Timestamp/1000, 0).Format(time.RFC3339)
1051: 		}
1052: 	}
1053: 
1054: 	// Apply time and project filters for non-session agents.
1055: 	// NOTE: OpenCode sessions are filtered EARLY (after ListSessions call) for performance.
1056: 	// This late filter catches:
1057: 	// 1. Tmux-only agents (not in OpenCode sessions)
1058: 	// 2. Completed workspaces (timestamps from workspace metadata, not session)
1059: 	if sinceDuration > 0 || len(projectFilterParam) > 0 {
1060: 		filtered := make([]AgentAPIResponse, 0, len(agents))
1061: 		for _, agent := range agents {
1062: 			// Time filter: check updated_at or spawned_at
1063: 			if sinceDuration > 0 {
1064: 				var agentTime time.Time
1065: 				if agent.UpdatedAt != "" {
1066: 					agentTime, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, agent.UpdatedAt)
1067: 				} else if agent.SpawnedAt != "" {
1068: 					agentTime, _ = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, agent.SpawnedAt)
1069: 				}
1070: 				if !agentTime.IsZero() && !filterByTime(agentTime, sinceDuration) {
1071: 					continue
1072: 				}
1073: 			}
1074: 
1075: 			// Project filter: check project name against all filters
1076: 			// Use Project field (derived from beads or workspace) instead of ProjectDir
1077: 			// because cross-project agents have ProjectDir=orchestrator-cwd, Project=target-project
1078: 			if len(projectFilterParam) > 0 {
1079: 				// Get project name to match
1080: 				projectName := agent.Project
1081: 				if projectName == "" && agent.ProjectDir != "" {
1082: 					// Fallback: extract project name from directory path
1083: 					projectName = extractProjectName(agent.ProjectDir)
1084: 				}
1085: 
1086: 				// Check if project name matches ANY filter
1087: 				matched := false
1088: 				for _, filter := range projectFilterParam {
1089: 					filterName := extractProjectName(filter) // Handle both "orch-go" and "/path/to/orch-go"
1090: 					if projectName == filterName {
1091: 						matched = true
1092: 						break
1093: 					}
1094: 				}
1095: 				if !matched {
1096: 					continue
1097: 				}
1098: 			}
1099: 
1100: 			filtered = append(filtered, agent)
1101: 		}
1102: 		agents = filtered
1103: 	}
1104: 
1105: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1106: 	if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(agents); err != nil {
1107: 		http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf("Failed to encode agents: %v", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)
1108: 		return
1109: 	}
1110: }

1112: // getProjectAPIPort returns the allocated API port for the current project.
1113: // Returns 0 if no allocation exists or on error.
1114: func getProjectAPIPort() int {

1116: 	if err != nil {
1117: 		return 0

1119: 	projectName := filepath.Base(projectDir)
1120: 
1121: 	registry, err := port.New("")

1134: // checkWorkspaceSynthesis checks if a workspace has a non-empty SYNTHESIS.md file.
1135: // This is used to detect completion for untracked agents (--no-track) where
1136: // there's no beads issue to check Phase: Complete.
1137: func checkWorkspaceSynthesis(workspacePath string) bool {
1138: 	if workspacePath == "" {
1139: 		return false
1140: 	}
1141: 	synthesisPath := filepath.Join(workspacePath, "SYNTHESIS.md")
1142: 	info, err := os.Stat(synthesisPath)
1143: 	if err != nil {
1144: 		return false
1145: 	}
1146: 	// SYNTHESIS.md must exist and be non-empty
1147: 	return info.Size() > 0
1148: }
1149: 
1150: // determineAgentStatus implements the Priority Cascade model for agent status.
1151: // This is the single source of truth for determining agent status.
1152: //
1153: // Priority order (highest to lowest):
1154: //  1. Beads issue closed → "completed" (orchestrator verified completion)
1155: //  2. Phase: Complete reported AND session dead → "awaiting-cleanup" (agent done, needs orch complete)
1156: //  3. Phase: Complete reported → "completed" (agent declared done, still has active session)
1157: //  4. SYNTHESIS.md exists AND session dead → "awaiting-cleanup" (artifact proves completion, needs cleanup)
1158: //  5. SYNTHESIS.md exists → "completed" (artifact proves completion)
1159: //  6. Session activity → sessionStatus ("active", "idle", or "dead")
1160: //
1161: // The "awaiting-cleanup" status distinguishes completed-but-orphaned agents from crashed agents.
1162: // This helps orchestrators prioritize: awaiting-cleanup needs orch complete, dead needs investigation.
1163: // See .kb/investigations/2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md for design rationale.
1164: // See .kb/investigations/2026-01-08-inv-handle-multiple-agents-same-beads.md for awaiting-cleanup addition.
1165: func determineAgentStatus(issueClosed bool, phaseComplete bool, workspacePath string, sessionStatus string) string {
1166: 	// Priority 1: Beads issue closed → completed (orchestrator verified completion)
1167: 	if issueClosed {
1168: 		return "completed"
1169: 	}
1170: 
1171: 	hasSynthesis := checkWorkspaceSynthesis(workspacePath)
1172: 	isDead := sessionStatus == "dead"
1173: 
1174: 	// Priority 2: Phase: Complete reported AND session dead → awaiting-cleanup
1175: 	// Agent finished work and reported completion, but orchestrator hasn't run orch complete.
1176: 	// This is NOT an error state - the agent did its job, just needs cleanup.
1177: 	if phaseComplete && isDead {
1178: 		return "awaiting-cleanup"
1179: 	}
1180: 
1181: 	// Priority 3: Phase: Complete reported (session still active/idle) → completed
1182: 	if phaseComplete {
1183: 		return "completed"
1184: 	}
1185: 
1186: 	// Priority 4: SYNTHESIS.md exists AND session dead → awaiting-cleanup
1187: 	// Agent wrote synthesis artifact (proof of completion) but session is dead.
1188: 	// Similar to Phase: Complete case - needs cleanup, not investigation.
1189: 	if hasSynthesis && isDead {
1190: 		return "awaiting-cleanup"
1191: 	}
1192: 
1193: 	// Priority 5: SYNTHESIS.md exists (session still active/idle) → completed
1194: 	if hasSynthesis {
1195: 		return "completed"
1196: 	}
1197: 
1198: 	// Priority 6: Session activity (fallback)
1199: 	// "dead" agents without completion signals truly need attention (crashed/stuck)
1200: 	return sessionStatus
1201: }

1203: // getGapAnalysisFromEvents reads spawn events and extracts gap analysis data for given beads IDs.
1204: // Returns a map of beads ID -> GapAPIResponse.
1205: func getGapAnalysisFromEvents(beadsIDs []string) map[string]*GapAPIResponse {
1206: 	result := make(map[string]*GapAPIResponse)
1207: 	if len(beadsIDs) == 0 {
1208: 		return result
1209: 	}
1210: 
1211: 	// Build a set of beads IDs for fast lookup
1212: 	beadsIDSet := make(map[string]bool)
1213: 	for _, id := range beadsIDs {
1214: 		beadsIDSet[id] = true
1215: 	}
1216: 
1217: 	// Read events file
1218: 	logPath := events.DefaultLogPath()
1219: 	file, err := os.Open(logPath)
1220: 	if err != nil {
1221: 		return result
1222: 	}
1223: 	defer file.Close()
1224: 
1225: 	// Scan events for spawn events matching our beads IDs
1226: 	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
1227: 	for scanner.Scan() {
1228: 		line := scanner.Text()
1229: 		if line == "" {
1230: 			continue
1231: 		}
1232: 
1233: 		var event events.Event
1234: 		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &event); err != nil {
1235: 			continue
1236: 		}
1237: 
1238: 		// Only process spawn events
1239: 		if event.Type != "session.spawned" {
1240: 			continue
1241: 		}
1242: 
1243: 		// Check if this event is for one of our beads IDs
1244: 		beadsID, ok := event.Data["beads_id"].(string)
1245: 		if !ok || !beadsIDSet[beadsID] {
1246: 			continue
1247: 		}
1248: 
1249: 		// Already have gap analysis for this beads ID? Skip (we want the most recent)
1250: 		// Since we read chronologically, later entries overwrite earlier ones
1251: 		if _, exists := result[beadsID]; exists {
1252: 			// We could skip, but let's allow overwrites for resumptions
1253: 		}
1254: 
1255: 		// Extract gap analysis data from event
1256: 		gapData := extractGapAnalysisFromEvent(event.Data)
1257: 		if gapData != nil {
1258: 			result[beadsID] = gapData
1259: 		}
1260: 	}
1261: 
1262: 	return result
1263: }
1264: 
1265: // extractGapAnalysisFromEvent extracts gap analysis data from a spawn event's data map.
1266: func extractGapAnalysisFromEvent(data map[string]interface{}) *GapAPIResponse {
1267: 	// Check if gap data exists
1268: 	hasGaps, ok := data["gap_has_gaps"].(bool)
1269: 	if !ok {
1270: 		return nil
1271: 	}
1272: 
1273: 	contextQuality := 0
1274: 	if cq, ok := data["gap_context_quality"].(float64); ok {
1275: 		contextQuality = int(cq)
1276: 	}
1277: 
1278: 	shouldWarn := false
1279: 	if sw, ok := data["gap_should_warn"].(bool); ok {
1280: 		shouldWarn = sw
1281: 	}
1282: 
1283: 	matchCount := 0
1284: 	if mc, ok := data["gap_match_total"].(float64); ok {
1285: 		matchCount = int(mc)
1286: 	}
1287: 
1288: 	constraints := 0
1289: 	if c, ok := data["gap_match_constraints"].(float64); ok {
1290: 		constraints = int(c)
1291: 	}
1292: 
1293: 	decisions := 0
1294: 	if d, ok := data["gap_match_decisions"].(float64); ok {
1295: 		decisions = int(d)
1296: 	}
1297: 
1298: 	investigations := 0
1299: 	if i, ok := data["gap_match_investigations"].(float64); ok {
1300: 		investigations = int(i)
1301: 	}
1302: 
1303: 	return &GapAPIResponse{
1304: 		HasGaps:        hasGaps,
1305: 		ContextQuality: contextQuality,
1306: 		ShouldWarn:     shouldWarn,
1307: 		MatchCount:     matchCount,
1308: 		Constraints:    constraints,
1309: 		Decisions:      decisions,
1310: 		Investigations: investigations,
1311: 	}
1312: }
1313: 
1314: // extractLastActivityFromMessages extracts the last meaningful activity from messages.
1315: // It looks for the most recent assistant message and extracts a summary of what
1316: // the agent is doing (tool use, text generation, etc.).
1317: // Returns nil if no activity can be extracted.
1318: func extractLastActivityFromMessages(messages []opencode.Message) *opencode.LastActivity {
1319: 	if len(messages) == 0 {
1320: 		return nil
1321: 	}
1322: 
1323: 	// Find the last assistant message (most relevant for activity)
1324: 	var lastAssistantMsg *opencode.Message
1325: 	for i := len(messages) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
1326: 		if messages[i].Info.Role == "assistant" {
1327: 			lastAssistantMsg = &messages[i]
1328: 			break
1329: 		}
1330: 	}
1331: 
1332: 	if lastAssistantMsg == nil {
1333: 		return nil
1334: 	}
1335: 
1336: 	// Extract activity from message parts
1337: 	// Priority: tool invocation > text > reasoning
1338: 	var activityText string
1339: 	for _, part := range lastAssistantMsg.Parts {
1340: 		switch part.Type {
1341: 		case "tool-invocation", "tool":
1342: 			// Tool use is the most informative activity
1343: 			activityText = "Using tool"
1344: 			if part.Text != "" {
1345: 				// Truncate tool text for display
1346: 				toolText := part.Text
1347: 				if len(toolText) > 40 {
1348: 					toolText = toolText[:40] + "..."
1349: 				}
1350: 				activityText = "Using tool: " + toolText
1351: 			}
1352: 		case "text":
1353: 			if part.Text != "" && activityText == "" {
1354: 				// Truncate long text
1355: 				text := part.Text
1356: 				if len(text) > 80 {
1357: 					// Find last space before 80 chars
1358: 					cutoff := 77
1359: 					for i := cutoff; i > 0; i-- {
1360: 						if text[i] == ' ' {
1361: 							cutoff = i
1362: 							break
1363: 						}
1364: 					}
1365: 					text = text[:cutoff] + "..."
1366: 				}
1367: 				activityText = text
1368: 			}
1369: 		case "reasoning":
1370: 			if activityText == "" {
1371: 				activityText = "Thinking..."
1372: 			}
1373: 		}
1374: 	}
1375: 
1376: 	if activityText == "" {
1377: 		return nil
1378: 	}
1379: 
1380: 	// Use message completion time if available, otherwise created time
1381: 	timestamp := lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Completed
1382: 	if timestamp == 0 {
1383: 		timestamp = lastAssistantMsg.Info.Time.Created
1384: 	}
1385: 
1386: 	return &opencode.LastActivity{
1387: 		Text:      activityText,
1388: 		Timestamp: timestamp,
1389: 	}
1390: }
1391: 
1392: // completionBacklogLastEmit tracks the last time we wrote completion_backlog metrics.
1393: // Rate-limits metric writes to at most once per 5 minutes to avoid spamming the
1394: // metrics file from dashboard polls (every 30s).
1395: var completionBacklogLastEmit time.Time
1396: 
1397: // globalStallTracker tracks token progress for stall detection.
1398: // Agents that are running but making no token progress for N minutes are flagged as stalled.
1399: // This catches agents stuck in infinite loops, crashed during tool execution, etc.
1400: var globalStallTracker = daemon.NewStallTracker(3 * time.Minute)
1401:

1403: // completion_backlog metrics to coaching-metrics.jsonl.
1404: // Rate-limited: writes at most once per 5 minutes.
1405: func emitCompletionBacklogMetrics(agents []AgentAPIResponse, phaseReportedAtMap map[string]time.Time) {
1406: 	// Rate limit: only emit once per 5 minutes
1407: 	if time.Since(completionBacklogLastEmit) < 5*time.Minute {
1408: 		return
1409: 	}

1443: 	metricsPath := coaching.DefaultMetricsPath()
1444: 	if metricsPath == "" {
1445: 		return
1446: 	}
1447: 
1448: 	for _, beadsID := range backlog {
1449: 		details := map[string]interface{}{
1450: 			"beads_id": beadsID,
1451: 		}
1452: 		if sid, ok := sessionMap[beadsID]; ok {
1453: 			details["session_id"] = sid
1454: 		}
1455: 		if reportedAt, ok := phaseReportedAtMap[beadsID]; ok {
1456: 			details["completed_at"] = reportedAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
1457: 			details["wait_minutes"] = int(time.Since(reportedAt).Minutes())
1458: 		}
1459: 
1460: 		m := coaching.Metric{
1461: 			Timestamp: time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
1462: 			SessionID: sessionMap[beadsID],
1463: 			Type:      "completion_backlog",
1464: 			Value:     float64(len(backlog)),
1465: 			Details:   details,
1466: 		}
1467: 		// Best-effort write; don't fail the API response on metric write errors
1468: 		_ = coaching.WriteMetric(metricsPath, m)
1469: 	}
1470: 
1471: 	completionBacklogLastEmit = time.Now()
1472: }

1474: // handleCacheInvalidate clears all dashboard caches to force fresh data on next request.
1475: // This is called by orch complete to ensure the dashboard shows updated agent status.
1476: // Without this, the TTL cache holds stale "active" status after agents complete.
1477: func handleCacheInvalidate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1478: 	if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
1479: 		http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
1480: 		return
1481: 	}
1482: 
1483: 	// Invalidate beads cache (open issues, all issues, comments)
1484: 	if globalBeadsCache != nil {
1485: 		globalBeadsCache.invalidate()
1486: 	}
1487: 
1488: 	// Invalidate beads stats cache (stats, ready issues)
1489: 	// Empty string clears all project caches
1490: 	if globalBeadsStatsCache != nil {
1491: 		globalBeadsStatsCache.invalidate("")
1492: 	}
1493: 
1494: 	// Invalidate workspace cache (workspace metadata)
1495: 	globalWorkspaceCacheInstance.invalidate()
1496: 
1497: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1498: 	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{
1499: 		"status":  "ok",
1500: 		"message": "Cache invalidated",
1501: 	})
1502: }

1504: // MessagePartResponse is the JSON structure for message parts in the activity feed.
1505: // This mirrors the SSEEvent structure used by the frontend for real-time events,
1506: // enabling seamless merging of historical API data with live SSE data.
1507: type MessagePartResponse struct {
1508: 	ID         string                `json:"id"`
1509: 	Type       string                `json:"type"` // Always "message.part" to match SSE event type
1510: 	Properties MessagePartProperties `json:"properties"`
1511: 	Timestamp  int64                 `json:"timestamp,omitempty"`
1512: }
1513: 
1514: // MessagePartProperties contains the part data in SSE-compatible format.
1515: type MessagePartProperties struct {
1516: 	SessionID string      `json:"sessionID"`
1517: 	MessageID string      `json:"messageID"`
1518: 	Part      PartDetails `json:"part"`
1519: }
1520: 
1521: // PartDetails contains the actual part content.
1522: type PartDetails struct {
1523: 	ID        string     `json:"id"`
1524: 	Type      string     `json:"type"` // "text", "tool", "reasoning", "step-start", "step-finish"
1525: 	Text      string     `json:"text,omitempty"`
1526: 	SessionID string     `json:"sessionID"`
1527: 	Tool      string     `json:"tool,omitempty"`
1528: 	State     *ToolState `json:"state,omitempty"`
1529: }
1530: 
1531: // ToolState contains tool invocation state for tool parts.
1532: type ToolState struct {
1533: 	Title  string                 `json:"title,omitempty"`
1534: 	Status string                 `json:"status,omitempty"`
1535: 	Input  map[string]interface{} `json:"input,omitempty"`
1536: 	Output string                 `json:"output,omitempty"`
1537: }
